stem:
The table below shows selected values of the linear function $f$.

| $x$ | $f(x)$ |
|-----|--------|
| 2   | 9      |
| 8   | 27     |

What is the slope of the graph of $f$ in the $xy$-plane?
explanation:
The slope of a linear function is the ratio of the change in $f(x)$ to the change in $x$ between any two points.

Using the two given points $(2, 9)$ and $(8, 27)$:
$$m = \frac{f(8) - f(2)}{8 - 2} = \frac{27 - 9}{8 - 2} = \frac{18}{6} = 3$$

The slope of the graph of $f$ is $\mathbf{3}$.

distractorRationale.A: Reads $f(2) = 9$ directly as the slope instead of computing the ratio of changes: the student treats the first output value as the slope.
distractorRationale.B: Inverts the slope formula, computing $\dfrac{\Delta x}{\Delta y} = \dfrac{8-2}{27-9} = \dfrac{6}{18} = \dfrac{1}{3}$ instead of $\dfrac{\Delta y}{\Delta x}$.
distractorRationale.D: Computes only the change in output values without dividing by the change in input: $27 - 9 = 18$, treating $\Delta f(x)$ alone as the slope.
